bioprojects.title: Puccinia striiformis f. sp. tritici strain:38S102 Genome sequencing and assembly

description.comment: Puccinia striiformis f. sp. tritici, causing stripe rust of wheat is a serious disease of wheat occurring in most wheat grown areas with cool and moist weather conditions. Puccinia striiformis f. sp. tritici is a macrocyclic, heteroecious fungus that requires living hosts to complete its life cycle. In this project whole genome sequencing of Puccinia striiformis f. sp. tritici Pathotype 38S102 was carried out. This pathotype was first isolated from Neelgiri Hills in 1973 and is avirulent to Yr9.
description.title: Microbe sample from Puccinia striiformis f. sp. tritici pathotype 38S102

assembly_info.comments: This is a diploid assembly of the wheat stripe rust fungus Puccinia striiformis f. sp. tritici strain 104 E137 A-. The contigs of the primary assembly are accessions PELB01000001-PELB01000156. The alternative haploid contigs (haplotigs) are accessions PELB01000157-PELB01000631

bioprojects.title: Puccinia striiformis f. sp. tritici PST-130 isolate:PST-130 alternate haplotype genome sequencing
Puccinia striiformis f. sp. tritici PST-130 Umbrella project

description.comment: PST-130 is equivalent to PSTv-69 in the new Pst nomenclature, which has virulence to Yr6, Yr7, Yr8, Yr9, Yr10, Yr17, Yr27, Yr32, Yr43, Yr44, and YrExp2; and avirulence to Yr1, Yr5, Yr15, Yr24, YrSP, YrTr1, and Yr76 (YrTye). PST-130 is a highly virulent race that was first identified in the Western USA in 2007
